title: Ixeris parva
taxonomy: Ixeris parva belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the genus Ixeris. The species is native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. It is a small herbaceous plant with daisy-like flowers. The exact classification of Ixeris parva may also include its order, such as Asterales.

intro: Ixeris parva, also known as small dandelion or dwarf hawk's beard, is a small, herbaceous perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used in Asian medicine, Ixeris parva is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The plant contains a range of bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Ixeris parva is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, often in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being and support the treatment of various health conditions.
